div1.entryFree:
Range, vb. 1) tr. to dispose in order: the wide arch of the --d empire fall, Ant. I, 1, 34 (Ff raing'd).
2) intr. to be ranked: whatsoever comes athwart his affection --s evenly with mine, Ado II, 2, 7. the line and the predicament wherein you r. under this subtle king, H4A I, 3, 169. r. with humble livers in content, H8 II, 3, 20. bury all, which yet distinctly --s, in heaps and piles of ruin, Cor. III, 1, 206.
